steps.title: Identify the key action in the examples
steps.content: The examples showcase artists deliberately incorporating and combining Indigenous musical elements with other styles—throat singing mixed with electric music, powwow melodies blended with rock.

steps.title: Match the verb to this blending
steps.content:
Substitute each word into "artists ______ distinctions between Indigenous music and other styles" and test against the examples (throat singing mixed with electric music; powwow melodies blended with rock):

A. Disguise means to hide or conceal. If artists disguised the distinctions, the Indigenous elements would be hidden — but both examples highlight the Indigenous elements as recognizable and central.

B. Reject means to refuse to accept. "Artists reject distinctions" means they refuse to treat Indigenous and other styles as separate categories — exactly what blending them demonstrates. ✓

C. Replace means to substitute one thing for another. The artists aren't removing Indigenous music and putting something else in; they are combining styles.

D. Observe means to notice or keep. "Observe distinctions" would mean honoring the separation between styles — the opposite of what the examples show.

B is correct.
